revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:羽束師坐高御産日神社]] {{神社 |名称 = Hazukashi-ni-masu Takamimusubi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hazukashi-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = [[Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Kyoto]] [[Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku]], Hazukashi-Shimizu-chō 219-1 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 55 | 緯度秒 = 46.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 23.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[Takamimusubi]]
[[Kamimusubi]] |社格 = [[Engishiki|Shikinai-sha]] (major)
Former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Gō-sha]] |創建 = Reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|1=Emperor Yūryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q357235}}, 21st year |本殿 = |別名 = Hazukashi Shrine (popular name) |例祭 = Annual Grand Festival (first Sunday in May) |神事 = }} [[File:Hazukashi-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Hazukashi-ni-masu Takamimusubi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|はづかしにますたかみむすびじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku]], [[Kyoto]]. It is listed as a major {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11064932}} in the ''[[Engishiki]]'', and under the modern shrine-ranking system it was classified as a [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|gō-sha]]. It is commonly known as “Hazukashi Shrine.” == Enshrined deities == * '''[[Takamimusubi]]''' (高皇産霊神) * '''[[Kamimusubi]]''' (神皇産霊神) == History == According to the ''Hazukashi-sha Kyūki'', compiled in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by the shrine priest Furukawa Taketake, the shrine was founded in the 21st year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|1=Emperor Yūryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q357235}}’s reign.{{Cite web |title=京都府神社略記 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1228340/1/50?keyword=%E7%BE%BD%E6%9D%9F%E5%B8%AB%E5%9D%90%E9%AB%98%E5%BE%A1%E7%94%A3%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2025-06-09 |publisher=National Diet Library |year=1936 |language=ja |author=Kyoto Prefectural Association of Shinto Priests}} In 567 (Kinmei 28) the shrine buildings reportedly survived a major flood of the [[Katsura River]] that devastated nearby settlements, whereupon the emperor granted the shrine fief households. In 665 (Tenchi 4) {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|ja|藤原鎌足|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q433995}}, acting on an imperial command, rebuilt the sanctuary. The surrounding area—though low-lying—lies at the confluence of the [[Katsura River]] and the former Obata River, and prospered from agriculture and river transport. Alongside the [[Kamo clan|Kamo]] and [[Hata clan|Hata]] peoples, the local occupational group {{ill|泊橿部|ja|泊橿部}} (“Hatukashi-be”) is thought to have lived here. ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' records that in Emperor Suinin 39 (legendary date) “ten groups of craftsmen including the Hatukashi-be were granted to {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}.” The legal commentary ''[[Ryō no shūge]]'' on the ''Shokuin-ryō'' notes that “Hatukashi-be” refers to the ancient '''波都加此''' clan. The shrine’s first secure textual appearance is in ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': on 3 April 701 (Taihō 1) an entry directs that the sacred rice offered to {{ill|Tsukiyomi Shrine (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516217|lt=Tsukiyomi Shrine|1=Tsukiyomi Shrine (Kyoto)}}, {{ill|Kabai Shrine|ja|樺井神}}, {{ill|Konoshima Shrine|ja|木嶋神}} and '''Hatugashi Shrine''' be henceforth supplied by the [[Nakatomi clan]]. Later, in 808 (Daidō 3), {{ill|Ibe no Hironari|ja|斎部広成|1=Inbe no Hironari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500712}}, concerned about unrest throughout the provinces, petitioned {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇|1=Emperor Heizei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q353558}} to newly enshrine eleven deities headed by [[Amaterasu]] for the peace of the realm. ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that on 8 September 859 (Jōgan 1) “offerings were made by imperial envoy to Hazukashi Shrine to pray for favourable winds and rain.” In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' ''Jinmyōchō'' (catalogue of shrines) it is listed first among the shrines of [[Yamashiro Province]]: “Hazukashi-ni-masu Takamimusubi Shrine  Major  Tsukinami, Niiname.” == Grounds == The precinct and the surrounding woods are collectively known as the “Hazukashi Forest.” Despite rapid urbanisation, the grove preserves traces of the Katsura River’s historical landscape, and is designated a Municipal Historic Site of Kyoto City. The large [[camphor tree]] within the precinct is recognised as one of the “Trees of Ward Pride” in Fushimi Ward. == Sub-shrines == * '''Kitamuki Mikaeri Tenmangū''' : An off-site auxiliary shrine. Tradition holds that [[Sugawara no Michizane]], on his way to exile in [[Dazaifu]], visited Hazukashi Shrine and composed the poem : “Abandoned, my heavy thoughts grow ever more— : Perhaps that is why this place is called the Forest of Shame.” Because Michizane is said to have looked back toward the north at that moment, the shrine now faces north (“kitamuki”) and enshrines him as {{ill|Tenjin|ja|天満宮|1=Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q798838}}. == Access == ; Address * 219-1 Hazukashi-Shimizu-chō, [[Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan ; Transport * Approx. 3 min walk from Kyoto City Bus “Hishikawa” or “Hijimeguchi” stops. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Hazukashi-jinja}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=569 Hazukashi-ni-masu Takamimusubi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Navi] (ja) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hazukashi-ni-masu Takamimusubi Shrine}} [[ja:羽束師坐高御産日神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65278999}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha]] [[Category:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
